hyperedges.label: Editorial Placements System: landing, calendarPage, culturePage, parisPage
Alpine Lazy-Load Architecture: entrypoint, $lazy plugin, component on-demand loading
Google Cloud Run Deployment Stack: frontend and backend
All content material controllers share the same CRUD pattern using services_firebase + utils_publication + utils_content_collections + utils_related_content
All migration/repair scripts operate on Firestore collections via firebase-admin with dry-run/apply pattern
Dynamic sitemap-content.xml must query all Firestore content collections to generate concrete discoverable URLs for Google

hyperedges.relation: Public landing read model aggregates content from multiple Firestore collections for different pages
All user CRUD routes protected by requireAuth middleware before reaching userController handlers
Stripe subscription lifecycle events update user subscription data in Firestore

hyperedges.label: Content Type Normalization Pattern
hyperedges.description: Both ContentCollectionContent and RelatedContent define the same set of content type fields (article, event, flipper, guide, interview, news, visualStory) and use identical normalizeIdList deduplication logic — they share the same data shape and normalization pattern for the same content types.

hyperedges.label: Alpine Global Stores Initialization
hyperedges.description: The Alpine entrypoint registers navbar, auth, and ui stores as global Alpine stores, making them accessible via $store in any Astro component that uses Alpine.

hyperedges.label: ArticleBody Block Type Dispatch
hyperedges.description: ArticleBody dispatches rendering of composite block types (two-columns, three-columns, video, flipper, one-big-one-small, collage) to specialized sub-components ArticleColumnContent and ArticleVideo using the RenderableBlock grouping pipeline.

All four creator logic functions implement the same Alpine.js content editor pattern: state management, Firebase Storage image upload, content block CRUD, author management, preview/save flow, and related content handling — differing only in content type and API endpoint.
All three components render paginated carousels of content cards with navigation, dot pagination, and tag labeling, acting as article page discovery widgets.

These four files form the interactive calendar feature: logic.ts provides Alpine.js state (date selection, event filtering, range mode), CalendarWidget renders the date grid, FilterButtons renders category filters, and EventList renders filtered event results — all driven by shared Alpine.js state via dispatched events.

hyperedges.label: Modal Composition Triad
hyperedges.description: ModalShell, ModalHeader, and ModalFooter form a composable modal system using DaisyUI dialog primitives and slot-based composition

hyperedges.label: Netlenka Card Variant Family
hyperedges.description: Four variants of an editorial card component (NetlenkaCard, One/Two/Three) all rendering article content with LazyImage and sharing the same prop interface

hyperedges.label: AlpineJS Store Consumer Components
hyperedges.description: Multiple UI components share state via AlpineJS $store (auth, navbar, ui.confirmation, alert storeName) enabling reactive UI without prop drilling

hyperedges.label: All Composer components share ContentCollectionsEditor, LandingPlacementPanel, DashboardMaterialActionFooter
ArticleComposer, EventComposer, GuideComposer all implement drag-and-drop content block editing
All Composer components use Alpine $lazy store pattern for reactive state initialization
Creator logic modules all manage author resolution, related content, content collections, firebase uploads, and landing placements via shared utilities
Section/page editor logic modules all call editorialPlacementsApi for updating landing and section page placements
Publication state UI components: banner shows status with toggle, indicator shows published dot, toggle is a checkbox input
Dashboard List Logic Delete Pattern
hyperedges.description: All three dashboard list logic modules share the same structural pattern: getUiStore, notify, buildApiUrl, confirmAndDelete, handleDeleteClick — all using Alpine UI store for confirmation dialogs and toast notifications before deleting a resource via REST API.

hyperedges.label: Article Hero Components Shared Pattern
hyperedges.description: GuideHero, InterviewHero, and TipsHero all share the same full-viewport hero layout with category label, title, lead text, ArticleAuthor, ArticleDate, and optional image with caption. They differ only in specific props and layout column ordering.

hyperedges.label: About Page EN Content Files
hyperedges.description: All six JSON content files under src/content/aboutPage/EN/ constitute the structured content data for the About page in English, covering page header, our story, stats, stats cards section, cards section, and why-choose-us sections.

hyperedges.label: EN Homepage Content Sections
Tag Taxonomy Data Sources
Multilingual Navbar and Article Content
All content-type API modules call request() with CRUD endpoints
fetchPublicContentPools/fetchRelatedContentLists aggregate all content-type APIs
Calendar page SSR pipeline: publicCalendarApi -> calendarPage utils -> Alpine lazy calendar component
Legal Pages Sharing LegalPageLayout
Culture Editor fetches multiple content APIs in parallel
Sitemap Content XML aggregates all content APIs
Preview pages share DashboardArticleBodyPreview for article body rendering
Landing editor aggregates all content APIs in a single Promise.all call
Le Saviez-Vous CRUD pages reuse ArticleComposer with articleType discriminator
Dashboard CRUD Flow: index + create + edit + preview pages share the same content management pattern
Public detail pages (news, events, tips, guide, interviews, flipper, visual-story, photo) all share the structured data + Layout + ContentActions SEO pattern
News, interviews, and flipper detail pages all use fetchPublicContentPools + buildContentCollectionItems to drive related content sidebars

hyperedges.description: Dashboard material audit documentation trio: README is entry point, IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN governs work order, IMPLEMENTATION_ROADMAP holds target contracts
Maag France legal documents trio that cross-reference each other and all cover Google Analytics usage
Materials with high-risk author restore pattern: selectedAuthorId is saved to preview draft but then overwritten from entity authorId on restore

hyperedges.label: Social Media Icon Set
Team Member Photos Collection
Navigation Arrow Icons Pair

nodes.label: Page Composition vs Content Metadata Separation
nodes.norm_label: page composition vs content metadata separation

nodes.label: sitemap-content.xml Dynamic Sitemap
nodes.rationale: Static Astro sitemap cannot discover API-backed Firestore content IDs; a separate dynamic endpoint fetches published content and generates concrete URLs for Google.

nodes.label: Structured Data JSON-LD Strategy
nodes.rationale: JSON-LD helps search engines understand entities, page types, article authorship, dates, publisher identity, breadcrumbs and events without changing visible markup.

nodes.label: robots.txt Runtime Route
nodes.rationale: Custom runtime robots.txt kept instead of astro-robots-txt package to avoid a new dependency for minimal gain; SSR Express wrapper makes build-time generation redundant.

nodes.label: Stale transitionAliases Dead Code
nodes.rationale: transitionAliases with landing-body-main-article-image-* were created for an old broken LandingBody namespace; LandingBody now uses canonical names so the aliases are orphaned dead code on 4 destination pages.

nodes.label: latestInterview vs hotContentItems Duplicate Transition Risk
nodes.rationale: If an interview has isLandingHotContentItem AND is interviews[0], it appears in both LatestInterview and NetlenkaCardOne with the same transition:name causing unpredictable browser animation.
nodes.norm_label: latestinterview vs hotcontentitems duplicate transition risk

nodes.label: visual-story destination missing transition:name
nodes.rationale: Source pages apply getTransitionName for visual stories but the destination page visual-story/[id].astro has no hero transition:name so no morph animation plays on navigation.

nodes.norm_label: photo of the day list logic
